summary.root_cause: The frozen manifest was generated from Windows working-tree JSON files with CRLF line endings, but the initial Git commit stored those JSON files as LF-normalized blobs because .gitattributes forced *.json text eol=lf. GitHub Actions checked out the LF-normalized blobs, so byte-level SHA-256 validation failed even though parsed JSON content was unchanged.
summary.resolution_option: OPTION A - restore/preserve the authoritative original bytes referenced by the frozen manifest
summary.notes: The two authoritative Windows working-tree files already matched the hashes recorded in artifacts/final_release/frozen_system_manifest.json. The repair preserves those exact bytes in Git by exempting only the two immutable frozen JSON records from line-ending normalization.

components.first_known_modification: Commit 0ad84a7 introduced the file as an LF-normalized Git blob while the frozen manifest expected the CRLF working-tree bytes generated before Git normalization.
components.authoritative_original_source: Existing Windows working-tree file bytes matched the frozen manifest SHA-256 exactly.
components.recommended_resolution: Preserve the exact frozen CRLF bytes and prevent Git line-ending normalization for this immutable record.
components.final_resolution: .gitattributes now treats this file as a binary frozen record and unsets eol normalization; the file is re-added byte-for-byte without editing the manifest.
